"""Shared utilities for PR commands. This module contains common functionality used by multiple PR commands (rewrite, submit) to avoid code duplication. """ from __future__ import annotations from dataclasses import dataclass from pathlib import Path import click from erk.core.commit_message_generator import ( CommitMessageGenerator, CommitMessageRequest, CommitMessageResult, ) from erk.core.context import ErkContext from erk.core.plan_context_provider import PlanContext from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import find_metadata_block, render_metadata_block from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import LIFECYCLE_STAGE from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_footer import build_pr_body_footer from erk_shared.gateway.gt.events import CompletionEvent, ProgressEvent from erk_shared.gateway.pr.diff_extraction import execute_diff_extraction from erk_shared.plan_store.conversion import header_str from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import build_original_plan_section from erk_shared.plan_store.types import PlanNotFound # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Branch Discovery # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- @dataclass(frozen=True) class BranchDiscovery: """Result of discovering branch context for PR commands.""" current_branch: str repo_root: Path trunk_branch: str parent_branch: str def discover_branch_context(ctx: ErkContext, *, cwd: Path) -> BranchDiscovery: """Discover branch context: current branch, repo root, trunk, and parent. Raises: click.ClickException: If in detached HEAD state """ current_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(cwd) if current_branch is None: raise click.ClickException("Not on a branch (detached HEAD state)") repo_root = ctx.git.repo.get_repository_root(cwd) trunk_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo_root) parent_branch = ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo_root, current_branch) or trunk_branch return BranchDiscovery( current_branch=current_branch, repo_root=repo_root, trunk_branch=trunk_branch, parent_branch=parent_branch, ) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Diff Extraction # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def run_diff_extraction( ctx: ErkContext, *, cwd: Path, session_id: str, base_branch: str, debug: bool, ) -> Path | None: """Run diff extraction with progress rendering. Wraps execute_diff_extraction() with event loop processing. Args: ctx: ErkContext providing gateways cwd: Current working directory session_id: Session ID for scratch file isolation base_branch: Branch to diff against debug: Whether to show progress output Returns: Path to diff file, or None if extraction failed """ result: Path | None = None for event in execute_diff_extraction( ctx, cwd, pr_number=0, session_id=session_id, base_branch=base_branch ): if isinstance(event, ProgressEvent): if debug: render_progress(event) elif isinstance(event, CompletionEvent): result = event.result return result # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Plan Context Display # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def echo_plan_context_status(plan_context: PlanContext | None) -> None: """Echo plan context status to the CLI.""" if plan_context is not None: click.echo( click.style( f" Incorporating plan #{plan_context.plan_id}", fg="green", ) ) if plan_context.objective_summary is not None: click.echo(click.style(f" Linked to {plan_context.objective_summary}", fg="green")) else: click.echo(click.style(" No linked plan found", dim=True)) click.echo("") # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Lifecycle Stage # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- _STAGES_BEFORE_IMPL = {None, "prompted", "planning", "planned"} def maybe_advance_lifecycle_to_impl( ctx: ErkContext, *, repo_root: Path, plan_id: str, quiet: bool, ) -> None: """Advance a linked plan's lifecycle_stage to "impl" if not already there. Intended for use after PR submission or rewrite so that plans are correctly marked as being implemented even when the user bypasses the /erk:plan-implement pipeline. Silently returns on any failure — lifecycle updates must never block submission. """ plan_result = ctx.plan_backend.get_plan(repo_root, plan_id) if isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound): return current_stage = header_str(plan_result.header_fields, LIFECYCLE_STAGE) if current_stage not in _STAGES_BEFORE_IMPL: return try: ctx.plan_backend.update_metadata(repo_root, plan_id, {"lifecycle_stage": "impl"}) except RuntimeError as e: if not quiet: msg = f" Warning: failed to update lifecycle stage: {e}" click.echo(click.style(msg, fg="yellow")) return if not quiet: click.echo(click.style(" Plan lifecycle stage updated to impl", dim=True)) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Cleanup # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def cleanup_diff_file(diff_file: Path | None) -> None: """Clean up a temporary diff file if it exists.""" if diff_file is not None and diff_file.exists(): try: diff_file.unlink() except OSError: pass # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # PR Body Assembly # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def build_plan_details_section(plan_context: PlanContext) -> str: """Build a collapsed
section embedding the plan in the PR body.""" issue_num = plan_context.plan_id parts = [ "", "## Implementation Plan", "", "
", f"Implementation Plan (Plan #{issue_num})", "", plan_context.plan_content, "", "
", ] return "\n".join(parts) def assemble_pr_body( *, body: str, plan_context: PlanContext | None, pr_number: int, header: str, existing_pr_body: str, ) -> str: """Assemble final PR body with plan details and footer. Args: body: AI-generated body content plan_context: Optional plan context for
section pr_number: PR number for footer checkout command header: Existing PR header to preserve (may be empty) existing_pr_body: Full PR body captured before gt submit overwrites it. Used to extract plan-header metadata block. When the extracted block is non-empty, uses original-plan details format instead of issue-based plan details format. Returns: Complete PR body ready for GitHub API """ plan_header = find_metadata_block(existing_pr_body, "plan-header") pr_body_content = body if plan_context is not None: if plan_header is not None: # Draft PR: use original-plan format (from lifecycle module) pr_body_content = body + build_original_plan_section(plan_context.plan_content) else: # Issue-based: use existing format pr_body_content = body + build_plan_details_section(plan_context) footer = build_pr_body_footer(pr_number) # Place metadata and header below content, above footer suffix = "" if plan_header is not None: suffix = "\n\n" + render_metadata_block(plan_header) if header: suffix = "\n\n" + header.rstrip("\n") + suffix return pr_body_content + suffix + footer def render_progress(event: ProgressEvent) -> None: """Render a progress event to the CLI.""" message = f" {event.message}" if event.style == "info": click.echo(click.style(message, dim=True)) elif event.style == "success": click.echo(click.style(message, fg="green")) elif event.style == "warning": click.echo(click.style(message, fg="yellow")) elif event.style == "error": click.echo(click.style(message, fg="red")) else: click.echo(message) def require_claude_available(ctx: ErkContext) -> None: """Verify Claude CLI is available, raising ClickException if not. Args: ctx: ErkContext providing prompt_executor Raises: click.ClickException: If Claude CLI is not available """ if not ctx.prompt_executor.is_available(): raise click.ClickException( "Claude CLI not found\n\nInstall from: https://claude.com/download" ) def run_commit_message_generation( *, generator: CommitMessageGenerator, diff_file: Path, repo_root: Path, current_branch: str, parent_branch: str, commit_messages: list[str] | None, plan_context: PlanContext | None, debug: bool, ) -> CommitMessageResult: """Run commit message generation and return result. Args: generator: CommitMessageGenerator instance diff_file: Path to the diff file repo_root: Repository root path current_branch: Current branch name parent_branch: Parent branch name commit_messages: Optional list of existing commit messages for context plan_context: Optional plan context from linked erk-plan issue debug: Whether to show debug output (currently unused, progress always shown) Returns: CommitMessageResult with the generated title/body or error info """ result: CommitMessageResult | None = None for event in generator.generate( CommitMessageRequest( diff_file=diff_file, repo_root=repo_root, current_branch=current_branch, parent_branch=parent_branch, commit_messages=commit_messages, plan_context=plan_context, ) ): if isinstance(event, ProgressEvent): render_progress(event) elif isinstance(event, CompletionEvent): result = event.result if result is None: return CommitMessageResult( success=False, title=None, body=None, error_message="Commit message generation did not complete", ) return result

content.content:
1→---
     2→description: Implement a plan from GitHub, file path, current branch, or current .erk/impl-context/ folder
     3→argument-hint: "[<plan-number-or-url-or-path>]"
     4→---
     5→
     6→# /erk:plan-implement
     7→
     8→Implement a plan - either from GitHub, a markdown file, an existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`, or by saving the current plan first.
     9→
    10→This is the primary implementation workflow - it orchestrates:
    11→
    12→1. Setting up the impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` (from issue, file, existing folder, or fresh plan)
    13→2. Executing the implementation
    14→3. Running CI and submitting the PR
    15→
    16→## Prerequisites
    17→
    18→- Must be in a git repository managed by erk
    19→- GitHub CLI (`gh`) must be authenticated
    20→- One of:
    21→  - A plan number, URL, or file path argument
    22→  - An existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`
    23→  - A plan branch checked out (e.g., `plnd/...` or `P{number}-...`)
    24→  - A plan in `~/.claude/plans/` (from plan mode)
    25→
    26→## Usage
    27→
    28→```bash
    29→/erk:plan-implement                    # Use impl directory, detect from branch, or save current plan
    30→/erk:plan-implement 2521               # Fetch and implement plan #2521
    31→/erk:plan-implement https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521   # URL form
    32→/erk:plan-implement ./my-plan.md       # Implement from local markdown file
    33→```
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Agent Instructions
    38→
    39→### Step 1: Set Up Implementation
    40→
    41→Parse `$ARGUMENTS` and run the consolidated setup command:
    42→
    43→- **If numeric** (e.g., `2521`): `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521`
    44→- **If GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/.../issues/2521`): Extract number, `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521`
    45→- **If path to file** (anything else non-empty): `erk exec setup-impl --file <path>`
    46→- **If empty**: `erk exec setup-impl` (auto-detects from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fails)
    47→
    48→```bash
    49→erk exec setup-impl [--issue <N> | --file <path>]
    50→```
    51→
    52→This single command handles:
    53→
    54→- Fetching plan from GitHub (draft PR)
    55→- Setting up from a local markdown file
    56→- Auto-detecting from existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`
    57→- Auto-detecting plan number from branch name (P{number}-... or PR lookup)

    76→

    79→Generate a branch slug from the plan title (the first `# ` heading in the plan file):
    80→
    81→- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters

    85→
    86→Save the current plan to GitHub and capture the plan number:

    99→
   100→If this fails, display the error and stop.
   101→
   102→### Step 2: Read Plan and Load Context
   103→

   105→

   167→
   168→This reads plan reference from the impl directory, captures session info, and uploads for async learn processing.
   169→
   170→### Step 11: Run CI Iteratively
   171→
   172→1. If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions
   173→2. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands
   174→
   175→**CRITICAL**: Never delete the impl directory - leave for user review (no auto-commit).
   176→
   177→### Step 12: Submit PR
   178→
   179→Push the branch and create or update the PR using the Graphite-aware submit pipeline:
   180→
   181→```bash
   182→erk pr submit
   183→```
   184→
   185→This handles pushing commits, creating/updating the PR, generating the PR description, and enhancing with Graphite stack metadata when available.
   186→

   188→
   189→```bash
   190→erk exec impl-signal submitted 2>/dev/null || true

   192→
   193→Then validate PR completion invariants:
   194→
   195→```bash

   198→
   199→This validates PR submission readiness including implementation-specific checks
   200→(e.g., `.erk/impl-context/` must be cleaned up). If checks fail, display output and warn user.
   201→
   202→### Step 13: Output Format
   203→
   204→- **Start**: "Setting up implementation..." or "Fetching plan #X..."
   205→- **After setup**: "Implementation environment ready, reading plan..."
   206→- **Each phase**: "Phase X: [brief description]" with code changes
   207→- **End**: "Plan execution complete. [Summary]"

file.content:
---
description: Implement a plan from GitHub, file path, current branch, or current .erk/impl-context/ folder
argument-hint: "[<plan-number-or-url-or-path>]"
---

# /erk:plan-implement

Implement a plan - either from GitHub, a markdown file, an existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`, or by saving the current plan first.

This is the primary implementation workflow - it orchestrates:

1. Setting up the impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` (from issue, file, existing folder, or fresh plan)
2. Executing the implementation
3. Running CI and submitting the PR

## Prerequisites

- Must be in a git repository managed by erk
- GitHub CLI (`gh`) must be authenticated
- One of:
  - A plan number, URL, or file path argument
  - An existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`
  - A plan branch checked out (e.g., `plnd/...` or `P{number}-...`)
  - A plan in `~/.claude/plans/` (from plan mode)

## Usage

```bash
/erk:plan-implement                    # Use impl directory, detect from branch, or save current plan
/erk:plan-implement 2521               # Fetch and implement plan #2521
/erk:plan-implement https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521   # URL form
/erk:plan-implement ./my-plan.md       # Implement from local markdown file
```

---

## Agent Instructions

### Step 1: Set Up Implementation

Parse `$ARGUMENTS` and run the consolidated setup command:

- **If numeric** (e.g., `2521`): `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521`
- **If GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/.../issues/2521`): Extract number, `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521`
- **If path to file** (anything else non-empty): `erk exec setup-impl --file <path>`
- **If empty**: `erk exec setup-impl` (auto-detects from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fails)

```bash
erk exec setup-impl [--issue <N> | --file <path>]
```

This single command handles:

- Fetching plan from GitHub (draft PR)
- Setting up from a local markdown file
- Auto-detecting from existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`
- Auto-detecting plan number from branch name (P{number}-... or PR lookup)
- Creating/checking out the feature branch
- Creating impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` with plan content
- Running impl-init validation
- Cleaning up `.erk/impl-context/` staging directory (git rm + commit + push)

Otherwise, generate a branch slug and set up from the specified issue:

1. Fetch the title: `gh pr view <ISSUE_ARG> --json title -q .title`
2. Generate a branch slug from the title:
   - 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters
   - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan)
   - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate
   - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
3. Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`.

```bash
erk exec setup-impl --issue <ISSUE_ARG> --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}"
```

If `setup-impl` exits with code 1 and `error: "no_plan_found"`, fall back to saving the current plan:

Generate a branch slug from the plan title (the first `# ` heading in the plan file):

- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters
- Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan)
- Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate
- Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`

Save the current plan to GitHub and capture the plan number:

```bash
erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}"
```

Parse the JSON output to get `plan_number`, then:

```bash
erk exec setup-impl --issue <plan_number>
```

The `setup-impl` output includes `related_docs` (skills and docs to load) and `has_plan_tracking` (whether GitHub issue tracking is active).

If this fails, display the error and stop.

### Step 2: Read Plan and Load Context

Read `plan.md` from the impl directory set up in Step 1 to understand:

- Overall goal and context
- Context & Understanding sections (API quirks, architectural insights, pitfalls)
- Implementation phases and dependencies
- Success criteria

**Context Consumption**: Plans contain expensive discoveries. Ignoring `[CRITICAL:]` tags, "Related Context:" subsections, or "DO NOT" items causes repeated mistakes.

### Step 3: Load Related Documentation

If plan contains "Related Documentation" section, load listed skills via Skill tool and read listed docs.

### Step 4: Create TodoWrite Entries

Create todo entries for each phase from the plan.

### Step 5: Signal GitHub Started

```bash
erk exec impl-signal started --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true
```

This also deletes the Claude plan file (from `~/.claude/plans/`) since:

- The content has been saved to GitHub
- The content has been snapshotted to `.erk/scratch/`
- Keeping it could cause confusion if the user tries to re-save

### Step 6: Execute Each Phase Sequentially

For each phase:

1. **Mark phase as in_progress** (in TodoWrite)
2. **Read task requirements** carefully
3. **Implement code AND tests together**:
   - Load `dignified-python-313` skill for coding standards
   - Load `fake-driven-testing` skill for test patterns
   - Follow project AGENTS.md standards
4. **Mark phase as completed** (in TodoWrite)
5. **Report progress**: changes made, what's next

**Important:** `plan.md` is immutable - NEVER edit during implementation

### Step 7: Report Progress

After each phase: report changes made and what's next.

### Step 8: Final Verification

Confirm all tasks executed, success criteria met, note deviations, summarize changes.

### Step 9: Signal GitHub Ended

```bash
erk exec impl-signal ended --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true
```

### Step 10: Upload Session for Async Learn

```bash
erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true
```

This reads plan reference from the impl directory, captures session info, and uploads for async learn processing.

### Step 11: Run CI Iteratively

1. If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions
2. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands

**CRITICAL**: Never delete the impl directory - leave for user review (no auto-commit).

### Step 12: Submit PR

Push the branch and create or update the PR using the Graphite-aware submit pipeline:

```bash
erk pr submit
```

This handles pushing commits, creating/updating the PR, generating the PR description, and enhancing with Graphite stack metadata when available.

After successful submission, signal lifecycle transition:

```bash
erk exec impl-signal submitted 2>/dev/null || true
```

Then validate PR completion invariants:

```bash
erk pr check --stage=impl
```

This validates PR submission readiness including implementation-specific checks
(e.g., `.erk/impl-context/` must be cleaned up). If checks fail, display output and warn user.

### Step 13: Output Format

- **Start**: "Setting up implementation..." or "Fetching plan #X..."
- **After setup**: "Implementation environment ready, reading plan..."
- **Each phase**: "Phase X: [brief description]" with code changes
- **End**: "Plan execution complete. [Summary]"

---

## Related Commands

- `/erk:plan-save` - Save plan only, don't implement (for defer-to-later workflow)
- `/erk:replan` - Re-plan an existing plan with current codebase state

1→"""Linear pipeline for PR submission.
     2→
     3→Replaces the dual-path branching orchestration with a sequence of plain
     4→functions transforming a frozen SubmitState dataclass.
     5→
     6→Each step: (ErkContext, SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError
     7→"""
     8→
     9→import dataclasses
    10→import sys
    11→import uuid
    12→from collections.abc import Callable
    13→from dataclasses import dataclass
    14→from functools import cache
    15→from pathlib import Path
    16→
    17→import click
    18→
    19→from erk.cli.commands.pr.shared import (
    20→    assemble_pr_body,
    21→    echo_plan_context_status,
    22→    maybe_advance_lifecycle_to_impl,
    23→    run_commit_message_generation,
    24→)
    25→from erk.cli.constants import PLANNED_PR_TITLE_PREFIX
    26→from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError
    27→from erk.core.commit_message_generator import CommitMessageGenerator
    28→from erk.core.context import ErkContext
    29→from erk.core.plan_context_provider import PlanContext, PlanContextProvider
    30→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PullRebaseError, PushError
    31→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound
    32→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    33→    extract_metadata_value,
    34→    extract_raw_metadata_blocks,
    35→    replace_metadata_block_in_body,
    36→)
    37→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.roadmap import (
    38→    rerender_comment_roadmap,
    39→    update_node_in_frontmatter,
    40→)
    41→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import parse_git_remote_url
    42→from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_footer import build_pr_body_footer
    43→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import BodyText, GitHubRepoId, PRNotFound
    44→from erk_shared.gateway.gt.operations.finalize import ERK_SKIP_LEARN_LABEL, is_learn_plan
    45→from erk_shared.gateway.gt.prompts import truncate_diff
    46→from erk_shared.gateway.pr.diff_extraction import filter_diff_excluded_files
    47→from erk_shared.gateway.pr.graphite_enhance import should_enhance_with_graphite
    48→from erk_shared.impl_folder import (
    49→    has_plan_ref,
    50→    read_plan_ref,
    51→    resolve_impl_dir,
    52→    save_plan_ref,
    53→    validate_plan_linkage,
    54→)
    55→from erk_shared.scratch.scratch import write_scratch_file
    56→
    57→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58→# Utilities
    59→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→
    61→
    62→def _add_planned_prefix(title: str) -> str:
    63→    """Prepend 'plnd/' prefix to PR title (idempotent).
    64→
    65→    Args:
    66→        title: The PR title to prefix
    67→
    68→    Returns:
    69→        The title with 'plnd/' prefix, skipping if already prefixed
    70→    """
    71→    if title.startswith(PLANNED_PR_TITLE_PREFIX):
    72→        return title
    73→    return f"{PLANNED_PR_TITLE_PREFIX}{title}"
    74→
    75→
    76→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→# Data Types
    78→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    79→
    80→
    81→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    82→class SubmitState:
    83→    """Immutable state threaded through the submit pipeline."""
    84→
    85→    cwd: Path
    86→    repo_root: Path
    87→    branch_name: str
    88→    parent_branch: str
    89→    trunk_branch: str
    90→    use_graphite: bool
    91→    force: bool
    92→    debug: bool
    93→    session_id: str
    94→    skip_description: bool
    95→    quiet: bool
    96→    plan_id: str | None
    97→    pr_number: int | None
    98→    pr_url: str | None
    99→    was_created: bool
   100→    base_branch: str | None
   101→    graphite_url: str | None
   102→    diff_file: Path | None
   103→    plan_context: PlanContext | None
   104→    title: str | None
   105→    body: str | None
   106→    existing_pr_body: str
   107→
   108→
   109→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   110→class SubmitError:
   111→    """Error result from a pipeline step."""
   112→
   113→    phase: str
   114→    error_type: str
   115→    message: str
   116→    details: dict[str, str]
   117→
   118→
   119→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   120→# Pipeline Steps
   121→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   122→
   123→SubmitStep = Callable[[ErkContext, SubmitState], SubmitState | SubmitError]
   124→
   125→
   126→def prepare_state(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   127→    """Resolve repo_root, branch_name, parent_branch, trunk_branch, plan_id.
   128→
   129→    Single location for all discovery. Consolidates duplicate parent-branch
   130→    and plan-id discovery sites.
   131→    """
   132→    cwd = state.cwd
   133→    repo_root = ctx.git.repo.get_repository_root(cwd)
   134→
   135→    branch_name = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(cwd)
   136→    if branch_name is None:
   137→        return SubmitError(
   138→            phase="prepare",
   139→            error_type="no_branch",
   140→            message="Not on a branch (detached HEAD state)",
   141→            details={},
   142→        )
   143→
   144→    trunk_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo_root)
   145→    parent_branch = ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo_root, branch_name) or trunk_branch
   146→
   147→    # Plan ID discovery via plan-ref.json in resolved .erk/impl-context/ dir
   148→    impl_dir = resolve_impl_dir(cwd, branch_name=branch_name)
   149→    plan_id: str | None = None
   150→    if impl_dir is not None:
   151→        try:
   152→            plan_id = validate_plan_linkage(impl_dir, branch_name)
   153→        except ValueError as e:
   154→            return SubmitError(
   155→                phase="prepare",
   156→                error_type="issue_linkage_mismatch",
   157→                message=str(e),
   158→                details={"branch": branch_name},
   159→            )
   160→
   161→    # Auto-repair: create plan-ref.json if missing but issue inferred from branch
   162→    if plan_id is not None and impl_dir is not None and not has_plan_ref(impl_dir):
   163→        remote_url = ctx.git.remote.get_remote_url(repo_root, "origin")
   164→        owner, repo_name = parse_git_remote_url(remote_url)
   165→        plan_url = f"https://github.com/{owner}/{repo_name}/pull/{plan_id}"
   166→        save_plan_ref(
   167→            impl_dir,
   168→            provider="github",
   169→            plan_id=plan_id,
   170→            url=plan_url,
   171→            labels=(),
   172→            objective_id=None,
   173→            node_ids=None,
   174→        )
   175→
   176→    return dataclasses.replace(
   177→        state,
   178→        cwd=cwd,
   179→        repo_root=repo_root,
   180→        branch_name=branch_name,
   181→        parent_branch=parent_branch,
   182→        trunk_branch=trunk_branch,
   183→        plan_id=plan_id,
   184→    )
   185→
   186→
   187→def commit_wip(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   188→    """Check for uncommitted changes; if present, add_all + commit."""
   189→    if ctx.git.status.has_uncommitted_changes(state.cwd):
   190→        if not state.quiet:
   191→            click.echo(click.style("   Committing uncommitted changes...", dim=True))
   192→        ctx.git.commit.add_all(state.cwd)
   193→        ctx.git.commit.commit(state.cwd, "WIP: Prepare for PR submission")
   194→    return state
   195→
   196→
   197→def capture_existing_pr_body(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   198→    """Capture full PR body before gt submit overwrites it."""
   199→    pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name)
   200→    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
   201→        return state
   202→    if not pr_result.body:
   203→        return state
   204→    return dataclasses.replace(state, existing_pr_body=pr_result.body)
   205→
   206→
   207→def push_and_create_pr(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   208→    """Push branch and create/find PR.
   209→
   210→    Dispatches internally: Graphite-first vs core path.
   211→    """
   212→    # Determine if Graphite should handle the push
   213→    graphite_handles_push = (
   214→        state.use_graphite and should_enhance_with_graphite(ctx, state.cwd).should_enhance
   215→    )
   216→
   217→    if graphite_handles_push:
   218→        return _graphite_first_flow(ctx, state)
   219→    return _core_submit_flow(ctx, state)
   220→
   221→
   222→def _graphite_first_flow(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   223→    """Graphite-first flow: gt submit handles push + PR creation."""
   224→    if not state.quiet:
   225→        click.echo(click.style("Phase 1: Graphite Submit", bold=True))
   226→
   227→    # Auto-force for plan implementations (branches always diverge from remote)
   228→    is_plan_impl = state.plan_id is not None
   229→    effective_force = state.force or is_plan_impl
   230→
   231→    # Pre-check: detect remote divergence before gt submit
   232→    if ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(state.repo_root, "origin", state.branch_name):
   233→        ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(state.repo_root, "origin", state.branch_name)
   234→        divergence = ctx.git.branch.is_branch_diverged_from_remote(
   235→            state.cwd, state.branch_name, "origin"
   236→        )
   237→        if divergence.behind > 0 and not effective_force:
   238→            ahead_msg = (
   239→                f" and ahead by {divergence.ahead} commit(s)" if divergence.ahead > 0 else ""
   240→            )
   241→            return SubmitError(
   242→                phase="push_and_create_pr",
   243→                error_type="remote_diverged",
   244→                message=(
   245→                    f"Branch '{state.branch_name}' is behind remote by "
   246→                    f"{divergence.behind} commit(s){ahead_msg}.\n\n"
   247→                    "The remote branch has been updated (e.g., by CI or another session).\n\n"
   248→                    "To fix:\n"
   249→                    "  erk pr reconcile-with-remote --dangerous  # Reconcile with remote\n"
   250→                    "  erk pr submit -f                          # Force push"
   251→                ),
   252→                details={
   253→                    "branch": state.branch_name,
   254→                    "ahead": str(divergence.ahead),
   255→                    "behind": str(divergence.behind),
   256→                },
   257→            )
   258→        if is_plan_impl and not state.force and divergence.behind > 0:
   259→            if not state.quiet:
   260→                click.echo(click.style("   Auto-forcing: plan implementation branch", dim=True))
   261→
   262→    if not state.quiet:
   263→        click.echo(click.style("   Running gt submit...", dim=True))
   264→    sys.stdout.flush()
   265→    try:
   266→        ctx.graphite.submit_stack(
   267→            state.repo_root,
   268→            publish=True,
   269→            restack=False,
   270→            quiet=state.quiet,
   271→            force=effective_force,
   272→        )
   273→    except RuntimeError as e:
   274→        error_str = str(e)
   275→        if "restack" in error_str.lower():
   276→            return SubmitError(
   277→                phase="push_and_create_pr",
   278→                error_type="graphite_restack_required",
   279→                message=(
   280→                    "Your Graphite stack has conflicts that need manual resolution.\n\n"
   281→                    "Run these commands:\n"
   282→                    "  gt restack        # Resolve conflicts interactively\n"
   283→                    "  erk pr submit     # Re-submit after resolving"
   284→                ),
   285→                details={},
   286→            )
   287→        return SubmitError(
   288→            phase="push_and_create_pr",
   289→            error_type="graphite_submit_failed",
   290→            message=f"Graphite submit failed: {e}",
   291→            details={},
   292→        )
   293→    if not state.quiet:
   294→        click.echo(click.style("   Graphite submit completed", fg="green"))
   295→        click.echo("")
   296→
   297→    # Query GitHub for PR info
   298→    if not state.quiet:
   299→        click.echo(click.style("   Getting PR info...", dim=True))
   300→    pr_info = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name)
   301→    if isinstance(pr_info, PRNotFound):
   302→        return SubmitError(
   303→            phase="push_and_create_pr",
   304→            error_type="pr_not_found",
   305→            message=(
   306→                f"PR not found for branch '{state.branch_name}' after gt submit.\n"
   307→                "This may happen if gt submit didn't create a PR. Try running:\n"
   308→                "  gt submit --publish"
   309→            ),
   310→            details={"branch": state.branch_name},
   311→        )
   312→
   313→    # Get Graphite URL
   314→    remote_url = ctx.git.remote.get_remote_url(state.repo_root, "origin")
   315→    owner, repo_name = parse_git_remote_url(remote_url)
   316→    repo_id = GitHubRepoId(owner=owner, repo=repo_name)
   317→    graphite_url = ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url(repo_id, pr_info.number)
   318→
   319→    if not state.quiet:
   320→        click.echo(click.style(f"   PR #{pr_info.number} ready", fg="green"))
   321→        click.echo("")
   322→
   323→    return dataclasses.replace(
   324→        state,
   325→        pr_number=pr_info.number,
   326→        pr_url=pr_info.url,
   327→        was_created=True,
   328→        base_branch=state.parent_branch,
   329→        graphite_url=graphite_url,
   330→    )

   333→def _core_submit_flow(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   334→    """Core submit flow: git push + gh pr create."""
   335→    if not state.quiet:
   336→        click.echo(click.style("Phase 1: Creating or Updating PR", bold=True))
   337→
   338→    # Check GitHub authentication
   339→    is_gh_authed, gh_username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
   340→    if not is_gh_authed:
   341→        return SubmitError(
   342→            phase="push_and_create_pr",
   343→            error_type="github_auth_failed",
   344→            message="GitHub CLI is not authenticated. Run 'gh auth login'.",
   345→            details={},
   346→        )
   347→    if state.debug and not state.quiet:
   348→        click.echo(click.style(f"   Authenticated as {gh_username}", dim=True))
   349→
   350→    # Verify commits to push
   351→    commit_count = ctx.git.analysis.count_commits_ahead(state.cwd, state.parent_branch)
   352→    if commit_count == 0:
   353→        return SubmitError(
   354→            phase="push_and_create_pr",
   355→            error_type="no_commits",
   356→            message=f"No commits ahead of {state.parent_branch}. Nothing to submit.",
   357→            details={"parent_branch": state.parent_branch, "branch": state.branch_name},
   358→        )
   359→
   360→    # Divergence check and auto-rebase
   361→    divergence = ctx.git.branch.is_branch_diverged_from_remote(
   362→        state.cwd, state.branch_name, "origin"
   363→    )
   364→    if divergence.behind > 0:
   365→        if state.debug and not state.quiet:
   366→            click.echo(
   367→                click.style(
   368→                    f"   Branch is {divergence.behind} commit(s) behind remote, rebasing...",
   369→                    dim=True,
   370→                )
   371→            )
   372→        pull_result = ctx.git.remote.pull_rebase(state.cwd, "origin", state.branch_name)
   373→        if isinstance(pull_result, PullRebaseError):
   374→            return SubmitError(
   375→                phase="push_and_create_pr",
   376→                error_type="rebase_failed",
   377→                message=(
   378→                    f"Failed to rebase '{state.branch_name}' on origin.\n"
   379→                    f"Error: {pull_result.message}\n\n"
   380→                    f"To fix manually: git pull --rebase origin {state.branch_name}"
   381→                ),
   382→                details={"branch": state.branch_name},
   383→            )
   384→        divergence = ctx.git.branch.is_branch_diverged_from_remote(
   385→            state.cwd, state.branch_name, "origin"
   386→        )
   387→
   388→    if divergence.is_diverged and not state.force:
   389→        return SubmitError(
   390→            phase="push_and_create_pr",
   391→            error_type="branch_diverged",
   392→            message=(
   393→                f"Branch '{state.branch_name}' has diverged from remote.\n"
   394→                f"Local is {divergence.ahead} commit(s) ahead and "
   395→                f"{divergence.behind} commit(s) behind origin/{state.branch_name}.\n\n"
   396→                f"To fix: git pull --rebase origin {state.branch_name}\n"
   397→                f"Or use: erk pr submit -f (to force push)"
   398→            ),
   399→            details={
   400→                "branch": state.branch_name,
   401→                "ahead": str(divergence.ahead),
   402→                "behind": str(divergence.behind),
   403→            },
   404→        )
   405→
   406→    # Push
   407→    push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
   408→        state.cwd, "origin", state.branch_name, set_upstream=True, force=state.force
   409→    )
   410→    if isinstance(push_result, PushError):
   411→        if "non-fast-forward" in push_result.message or "rejected" in push_result.message.lower():
   412→            return SubmitError(
   413→                phase="push_and_create_pr",
   414→                error_type="branch_diverged",
   415→                message=(
   416→                    f"Branch '{state.branch_name}' has diverged from remote.\n"
   417→                    f"Your local branch is behind origin/{state.branch_name}.\n\n"
   418→                    f"To fix: git pull --rebase origin {state.branch_name}\n"
   419→                    f"Or use: erk pr submit -f (to force push)"
   420→                ),
   421→                details={"branch": state.branch_name},
   422→            )
   423→        raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message)
   424→
   425→    # Check for existing PR or create new one
   426→    existing_pr = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name)
   427→
   428→    if isinstance(existing_pr, PRNotFound):
   429→        # Check parent branch has PR (stacked PRs)
   430→        if state.parent_branch != state.trunk_branch:
   431→            parent_pr = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(state.repo_root, state.parent_branch)
   432→            if isinstance(parent_pr, PRNotFound):
   433→                return SubmitError(
   434→                    phase="push_and_create_pr",
   435→                    error_type="parent_branch_no_pr",
   436→                    message=(
   437→                        f"Cannot create PR: parent branch '{state.parent_branch}' "
   438→                        f"does not have a PR yet.\n\n"
   439→                        f"This branch is part of a Graphite stack. Use 'gt submit' "
   440→                        f"to submit the entire stack at once, which will create PRs "
   441→                        f"for all branches in the correct order.\n\n"
   442→                        f"Run: gt submit -s"
   443→                    ),
   444→                    details={
   445→                        "branch": state.branch_name,
   446→                        "parent_branch": state.parent_branch,
   447→                    },
   448→                )
   449→
   450→        # Build initial PR body with footer
   451→        footer = build_pr_body_footer(0)
   452→        full_body = "" + footer
   453→
   454→        pr_number = ctx.github.create_pr(
   455→            state.repo_root,
   456→            branch=state.branch_name,
   457→            title="WIP",
   458→            body=full_body,
   459→            base=state.parent_branch,
   460→        )
   461→
   462→        # Get PR URL
   463→        pr_details = ctx.github.get_pr(state.repo_root, pr_number)
   464→        pr_url = (
   465→            pr_details.url
   466→            if not isinstance(pr_details, PRNotFound)
   467→            else f"https://github.com/{state.branch_name}/pull/{pr_number}"
   468→        )
   469→
   470→        # Update footer with actual PR number
   471→        updated_footer = build_pr_body_footer(pr_number)
   472→        ctx.github.update_pr_body(state.repo_root, pr_number, "" + updated_footer)
   473→
   474→        if not state.quiet:
   475→            click.echo(click.style(f"   PR #{pr_number} created", fg="green"))
   476→            click.echo("")
   477→
   478→        return dataclasses.replace(
   479→            state,
   480→            pr_number=pr_number,
   481→            pr_url=pr_url,
   482→            was_created=True,
   483→            base_branch=state.parent_branch,
   484→        )
   485→
   486→    # PR exists
   487→    pr_number = existing_pr.number
   488→    pr_url = existing_pr.url
   489→
   490→    # Add footer if missing
   491→    pr_details = ctx.github.get_pr(state.repo_root, pr_number)
   492→    current_body = "" if isinstance(pr_details, PRNotFound) else pr_details.body
   493→    has_footer = "erk pr checkout" in current_body
   494→    if not has_footer:
   495→        footer = build_pr_body_footer(pr_number)
   496→        ctx.github.update_pr_body(state.repo_root, pr_number, current_body + footer)
   497→
   498→    if not state.quiet:
   499→        click.echo(click.style(f"   PR #{pr_number} found (already exists)", fg="green"))
   500→        click.echo("")
   501→
   502→    return dataclasses.replace(
   503→        state,
   504→        pr_number=pr_number,
   505→        pr_url=pr_url,
   506→        was_created=False,
   507→        base_branch=state.parent_branch,
   508→    )

   511→def extract_diff(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   512→    """Local git diff to base branch, filter lock files, truncate, write scratch file."""
   513→    if state.skip_description:
   514→        return state
   515→    click.echo(click.style("Phase 2: Getting diff", bold=True))
   516→
   517→    if state.base_branch is None:
   518→        return SubmitError(
   519→            phase="extract_diff",
   520→            error_type="no_base_branch",
   521→            message="No base branch determined for diff extraction",
   522→            details={},
   523→        )
   524→
   525→    pr_diff = ctx.git.analysis.get_diff_to_branch(state.cwd, state.base_branch)
   526→    if state.debug:
   527→        diff_lines = len(pr_diff.splitlines())
   528→        click.echo(click.style(f"   Diff retrieved ({diff_lines} lines)", dim=True))
   529→
   530→    # Filter lock files, truncate
   531→    pr_diff = filter_diff_excluded_files(pr_diff)
   532→    diff_content, was_truncated = truncate_diff(pr_diff)
   533→    if was_truncated and state.debug:
   534→        click.echo(click.style("   Diff truncated for size", dim=True))
   535→
   536→    # Write scratch file
   537→    diff_file = write_scratch_file(
   538→        diff_content,
   539→        session_id=state.session_id,
   540→        suffix=".diff",
   541→        prefix="pr-diff-",
   542→        repo_root=state.repo_root,
   543→    )
   544→
   545→    click.echo("")
   546→
   547→    return dataclasses.replace(state, diff_file=diff_file)

   550→def fetch_plan_context(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   551→    """Fetch plan context from linked erk-plan issue."""
   552→    if state.skip_description:

   554→    click.echo(click.style("Phase 3: Fetching plan context", bold=True))
   555→
   556→    plan_provider = PlanContextProvider(
   557→        plan_backend=ctx.plan_backend, github_issues=ctx.github_issues
   558→    )
   559→    plan_context = plan_provider.get_plan_context(
   560→        repo_root=state.repo_root,
   561→        branch_name=state.branch_name,
   562→    )
   563→
   564→    echo_plan_context_status(plan_context)
   565→
   566→    return dataclasses.replace(state, plan_context=plan_context)

   569→def generate_description(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   570→    """Generate AI PR title and body via CommitMessageGenerator."""
   571→    if state.skip_description:
   572→        return state
   573→    click.echo(click.style("Phase 4: Generating PR description", bold=True))
   574→
   575→    if state.diff_file is None:
   576→        return SubmitError(
   577→            phase="generate_description",
   578→            error_type="no_diff_file",
   579→            message="Failed to extract diff for AI analysis",
   580→            details={},
   581→        )
   582→
   583→    # Get commit messages for AI context
   584→    commit_messages = ctx.git.commit.get_commit_messages_since(state.cwd, state.parent_branch)
   585→
   586→    msg_gen = CommitMessageGenerator(ctx.prompt_executor)
   587→    msg_result = run_commit_message_generation(
   588→        generator=msg_gen,
   589→        diff_file=state.diff_file,
   590→        repo_root=state.repo_root,
   591→        current_branch=state.branch_name,
   592→        parent_branch=state.parent_branch,
   593→        commit_messages=commit_messages,
   594→        plan_context=state.plan_context,
   595→        debug=state.debug,
   596→    )
   597→
   598→    if not msg_result.success:
   599→        return SubmitError(
   600→            phase="generate_description",
   601→            error_type="ai_generation_failed",
   602→            message=f"Failed to generate message: {msg_result.error_message}",
   603→            details={},
   604→        )
   605→
   606→    click.echo("")
   607→
   608→    return dataclasses.replace(
   609→        state,
   610→        title=msg_result.title or "Update",
   611→        body=msg_result.body or "",
   612→    )

   614→
   615→def enhance_with_graphite(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   616→    """No-op if graphite_url already set. Otherwise check auth + tracking, run gt submit."""
   617→    # Skip if Graphite already handled the push (graphite-first path)
   618→    if state.graphite_url is not None:
   619→        return state
   620→
   621→    # Skip if user disabled Graphite
   622→    if not state.use_graphite:
   623→        return state
   624→
   625→    click.echo(click.style("Phase 5: Graphite enhancement", bold=True))
   626→
   627→    if state.pr_number is None:
   628→        return state
   629→
   630→    # Check Graphite auth
   631→    is_gt_authed, gt_username, _ = ctx.graphite.check_auth_status()
   632→    if not is_gt_authed:
   633→        if state.debug:
   634→            click.echo(click.style("   Graphite not authenticated, skipping enhancement", dim=True))
   635→        click.echo("")
   636→        return state
   637→
   638→    # Check if branch is tracked
   639→    repo_root = state.repo_root
   640→    all_branches = ctx.graphite.get_all_branches(ctx.git, repo_root)
   641→    if state.branch_name not in all_branches:
   642→        if state.debug:
   643→            click.echo(
   644→                click.style("   Branch not tracked by Graphite, skipping enhancement", dim=True)
   645→            )
   646→        click.echo("")
   647→        return state
   648→
   649→    # Run gt submit
   650→    sys.stdout.flush()
   651→    try:
   652→        ctx.graphite.submit_stack(
   653→            repo_root,
   654→            publish=True,
   655→            restack=False,
   656→            quiet=state.quiet,
   657→            force=state.force,
   658→        )
   659→    except RuntimeError as e:
   660→        error_msg = str(e).lower()
   661→        if "nothing to submit" in error_msg or "no changes" in error_msg:
   662→            click.echo(click.style("   PR already up to date with Graphite", fg="green"))
   663→            click.echo("")
   664→            return state
   665→        return SubmitError(
   666→            phase="enhance_with_graphite",
   667→            error_type="graphite_enhance_failed",
   668→            message=f"Graphite enhancement failed: {e}",
   669→            details={},
   670→        )
   671→
   672→    # Get Graphite URL
   673→    remote_url = ctx.git.remote.get_remote_url(repo_root, "origin")
   674→    owner, repo_name = parse_git_remote_url(remote_url)
   675→    repo_id = GitHubRepoId(owner=owner, repo=repo_name)
   676→    graphite_url: str | None = None
   677→    if state.pr_number is not None:
   678→        graphite_url = ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url(repo_id, state.pr_number)
   679→
   680→    click.echo("")
   681→
   682→    return dataclasses.replace(state, graphite_url=graphite_url)
   683→
   684→
   685→def finalize_pr(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   686→    """Update PR title/body with footer, add labels, amend local commit, clean up diff file."""
   687→    if state.skip_description:
   688→        return state
   689→    click.echo(click.style("Phase 6: Updating PR metadata", bold=True))
   690→
   691→    if state.pr_number is None:
   692→        return SubmitError(
   693→            phase="finalize_pr",
   694→            error_type="no_pr_number",
   695→            message="No PR number available for finalization",
   696→            details={},
   697→        )
   698→
   699→    pr_body = state.body or ""
   700→    pr_title = state.title or "Update"
   701→
   702→    # Use pre-captured PR body (captured before gt submit overwrites it)
   703→    existing_pr_body = state.existing_pr_body
   704→
   705→    # Check learn plan label
   706→    impl_dir = resolve_impl_dir(state.cwd, branch_name=state.branch_name)
   707→    is_learn_origin = is_learn_plan(impl_dir) if impl_dir is not None else False
   708→
   709→    # Assemble PR body with plan details and footer
   710→    final_body = assemble_pr_body(
   711→        body=pr_body,
   712→        plan_context=state.plan_context,
   713→        pr_number=state.pr_number,
   714→        header="",
   715→        existing_pr_body=existing_pr_body,
   716→    )
   717→
   718→    # Update PR metadata
   719→    ctx.github.update_pr_title_and_body(
   720→        repo_root=state.repo_root,
   721→        pr_number=state.pr_number,
   722→        title=pr_title,
   723→        body=BodyText(content=final_body),
   724→    )
   725→
   726→    # Publish draft PR if needed
   727→    pr_draft_check = ctx.github.get_pr(state.repo_root, state.pr_number)
   728→    if not isinstance(pr_draft_check, PRNotFound) and pr_draft_check.is_draft:
   729→        click.echo(click.style("   Publishing draft PR...", dim=True))
   730→        ctx.github.mark_pr_ready(state.repo_root, state.pr_number)
   731→
   732→    # Add learn skip label if applicable
   733→    if is_learn_origin:
   734→        ctx.github.add_label_to_pr(state.repo_root, state.pr_number, ERK_SKIP_LEARN_LABEL)
   735→
   736→    # Amend local commit with title and body (without metadata footer)
   737→    commit_message = pr_title
   738→    if pr_body:
   739→        commit_message = f"{pr_title}\n\n{pr_body}"
   740→    ctx.git.commit.amend_commit(state.repo_root, commit_message)
   741→
   742→    # Fix Graphite tracking divergence caused by the amend
   743→    if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None:
   744→        ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name)
   745→
   746→    # Clean up temp diff file
   747→    if state.diff_file is not None and state.diff_file.exists():
   748→        try:
   749→            state.diff_file.unlink()
   750→        except OSError as e:
   751→            if state.debug:
   752→                click.echo(click.style(f"   Failed to clean up diff file: {e}", dim=True))
   753→
   754→    click.echo(click.style("   PR metadata updated", fg="green"))
   755→
   756→    # Update lifecycle stage for linked plan
   757→    plan_id_for_lifecycle: str | None = None
   758→    if state.plan_context is not None:
   759→        plan_id_for_lifecycle = state.plan_context.plan_id
   760→    elif state.pr_number is not None:
   761→        plan_id_for_lifecycle = str(state.pr_number)
   762→
   763→    if plan_id_for_lifecycle is not None:
   764→        maybe_advance_lifecycle_to_impl(
   765→            ctx,
   766→            repo_root=state.repo_root,
   767→            plan_id=plan_id_for_lifecycle,
   768→            quiet=state.quiet,
   769→        )
   770→
   771→    click.echo("")
   772→
   773→    # Get final PR URL
   774→    pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name)
   775→    pr_url = pr_result.url if not isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound) else state.pr_url or ""
   776→
   777→    # Get Graphite URL if not already set
   778→    graphite_url = state.graphite_url
   779→    if graphite_url is None and state.use_graphite:
   780→        is_gt_authed, _, _ = ctx.graphite.check_auth_status()
   781→        if is_gt_authed:
   782→            remote_url = ctx.git.remote.get_remote_url(state.repo_root, "origin")
   783→            owner, repo_name = parse_git_remote_url(remote_url)
   784→            repo_id = GitHubRepoId(owner=owner, repo=repo_name)
   785→            graphite_url = ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url(repo_id, state.pr_number)
   786→
   787→    return dataclasses.replace(
   788→        state,
   789→        pr_url=pr_url,
   790→        graphite_url=graphite_url,
   791→        title=pr_title,
   792→        body=pr_body,
   793→    )

   795→
   796→def link_pr_to_objective_nodes(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   797→    """Link the PR to objective roadmap nodes if .erk/impl-context/ contains node_ids."""
   798→    if state.pr_number is None:
   799→        return state
   800→
   801→    impl_dir = resolve_impl_dir(state.cwd, branch_name=state.branch_name)
   802→    if impl_dir is None:
   803→        return state
   804→
   805→    plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
   806→    if plan_ref is None:
   807→        return state
   808→
   809→    if plan_ref.objective_id is None or plan_ref.node_ids is None:
   810→        return state
   811→
   812→    if len(plan_ref.node_ids) == 0:
   813→        return state
   814→
   815→    objective_id = plan_ref.objective_id
   816→    pr_ref = f"#{state.pr_number}"
   817→
   818→    issue = ctx.github_issues.get_issue(state.repo_root, objective_id)
   819→    if isinstance(issue, IssueNotFound):
   820→        click.echo(
   821→            click.style(
   822→                f"   Warning: Objective #{objective_id} not found, skipping node linking",
   823→                dim=True,
   824→            )
   825→        )
   826→        return state
   827→
   828→    linked_nodes: list[str] = []
   829→
   830→    # Extract roadmap block once before iterating through node_ids
   831→    raw_blocks = extract_raw_metadata_blocks(issue.body)
   832→    roadmap_block = None
   833→    for block in raw_blocks:
   834→        if block.key == "objective-roadmap":
   835→            roadmap_block = block
   836→            break
   837→
   838→    updated_body = issue.body
   839→    if roadmap_block is not None:
   840→        block_content = roadmap_block.body
   841→        for node_id in plan_ref.node_ids:
   842→            updated_block_content = update_node_in_frontmatter(
   843→                block_content,
   844→                node_id,
   845→                pr=pr_ref,
   846→                status=None,
   847→            )
   848→            if updated_block_content is None:
   849→                continue
   850→
   851→            block_content = updated_block_content
   852→            linked_nodes.append(node_id)
   853→
   854→        if linked_nodes:
   855→            new_block_with_markers = (
   856→                "<!-- WARNING: Machine-generated. Manual edits may break erk tooling. -->\n"
   857→                "<!-- erk:metadata-block:objective-roadmap -->\n"
   858→                f"{block_content}\n"
   859→                "<!-- /erk:metadata-block:objective-roadmap -->"
   860→            )
   861→            try:
   862→                updated_body = replace_metadata_block_in_body(
   863→                    updated_body, "objective-roadmap", new_block_with_markers
   864→                )
   865→            except ValueError:
   866→                linked_nodes.clear()
   867→
   868→    if linked_nodes:
   869→        ctx.github_issues.update_issue_body(
   870→            state.repo_root, objective_id, BodyText(content=updated_body)
   871→        )
   872→
   873→        # Re-render comment roadmap table if applicable
   874→        objective_comment_id = extract_metadata_value(
   875→            updated_body, "objective-header", "objective_comment_id"
   876→        )
   877→        if objective_comment_id is not None:
   878→            comment_body = ctx.github_issues.get_comment_by_id(
   879→                state.repo_root, objective_comment_id
   880→            )
   881→            updated_comment = rerender_comment_roadmap(updated_body, comment_body)
   882→            if updated_comment is not None and updated_comment != comment_body:
   883→                ctx.github_issues.update_comment(
   884→                    state.repo_root, objective_comment_id, updated_comment
   885→                )
   886→
   887→        node_list = ", ".join(linked_nodes)
   888→        click.echo(
   889→            click.style(
   890→                f"   Linked PR #{state.pr_number} to objective #{objective_id} nodes: {node_list}",
   891→                fg="green",
   892→            )
   893→        )
   894→
   895→    return state
   896→
   897→
   898→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   899→# Helper
   900→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   901→
   902→
   903→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   904→# Pipeline Runner
   905→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   906→
   907→
   908→@cache
   909→def _push_and_create_pipeline() -> tuple[SubmitStep, ...]:
   910→    return (
   911→        prepare_state,
   912→        commit_wip,
   913→        capture_existing_pr_body,
   914→        push_and_create_pr,
   915→    )
   916→
   917→
   918→def run_push_and_create_pipeline(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   919→    """Run only the push-and-create portion of the submit pipeline."""
   920→    for step in _push_and_create_pipeline():
   921→        try:
   922→            result = step(ctx, state)
   923→        except Exception as e:
   924→            step_name = getattr(step, "__name__", repr(step))
   925→            return SubmitError(
   926→                phase=step_name,
   927→                error_type="unhandled_exception",
   928→                message=f"Unexpected error in {step_name}: {e}",
   929→                details={},
   930→            )
   931→        if isinstance(result, SubmitError):
   932→            return result
   933→        state = result
   934→    return state
   935→
   936→
   937→@cache
   938→def _submit_pipeline() -> tuple[SubmitStep, ...]:
   939→    return (
   940→        prepare_state,
   941→        commit_wip,
   942→        capture_existing_pr_body,
   943→        push_and_create_pr,
   944→        extract_diff,
   945→        fetch_plan_context,
   946→        generate_description,
   947→        enhance_with_graphite,
   948→        finalize_pr,
   949→        link_pr_to_objective_nodes,
   950→    )
   951→
   952→
   953→def run_submit_pipeline(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError:
   954→    """Run the submit pipeline, returning final state or first error."""
   955→    for step in _submit_pipeline():
   956→        try:
   957→            result = step(ctx, state)
   958→        except Exception as e:
   959→            step_name = getattr(step, "__name__", repr(step))
   960→            return SubmitError(
   961→                phase=step_name,
   962→                error_type="unhandled_exception",
   963→                message=f"Unexpected error in {step_name}: {e}",
   964→                details={},
   965→            )
   966→        if isinstance(result, SubmitError):
   967→            return result
   968→        state = result
   969→    return state
   970→
   971→
   972→def make_initial_state(
   973→    *,
   974→    cwd: Path,
   975→    use_graphite: bool,
   976→    force: bool,
   977→    debug: bool,
   978→    session_id: str | None,
   979→    skip_description: bool,
   980→    quiet: bool,
   981→) -> SubmitState:
   982→    """Create initial SubmitState with only CLI-provided values.
   983→
   984→    All discovery fields start as empty/None and are populated by prepare_state().
   985→    """
   986→    resolved_session_id = session_id if session_id is not None else str(uuid.uuid4())
   987→    return SubmitState(
   988→        cwd=cwd,
   989→        repo_root=cwd,
   990→        branch_name="",
   991→        parent_branch="",
   992→        trunk_branch="",
   993→        use_graphite=use_graphite,
   994→        force=force,
   995→        debug=debug,
   996→        session_id=resolved_session_id,
   997→        skip_description=skip_description,
   998→        quiet=quiet,
   999→        plan_id=None,
  1000→        pr_number=None,
  1001→        pr_url=None,
  1002→        was_created=False,
  1003→        base_branch=None,
  1004→        graphite_url=None,
  1005→        diff_file=None,
  1006→        plan_context=None,
  1007→        title=None,
  1008→        body=None,
  1009→        existing_pr_body="",
  1010→    )
  1011→
